commit.message:
[Evaluation] Fix model_config 404 for Foundry-style endpoints in red team (#45502)

* Fix model_config 404 for Foundry-style endpoints

Detect Foundry-style Azure endpoints (*.services.ai.azure.com) in
get_chat_target() and append /openai/v1 before passing to PyRIT's
OpenAIChatTarget. Without this, PyRIT constructs URLs like
https://resource.services.ai.azure.com/chat/completions which returns 404.
The correct URL path is /openai/v1/chat/completions.

Traditional Azure OpenAI endpoints (*.openai.azure.com) are not affected.

Fixes: Tests 1.10, 1.11, 1.13, 1.14

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add unit tests for Foundry endpoint URL normalization

Test that *.services.ai.azure.com endpoints get /openai/v1 appended,
double-append is prevented, trailing slashes are handled, and traditional
*.openai.azure.com endpoints are not modified.

Fix log slowdown, add scroll to bottom button, upload build artifact for testing (#2)

* Fix serial terminal slowdown and post-disconnect message flooding

- Batch serial data in 50ms intervals before sending to webview to
  prevent IPC message queue flooding at high baud rates
- Cancel pending flush and discard buffer on disconnect to prevent
  queued messages from draining for minutes after device is unplugged
- Use requestAnimationFrame for autoscroll to avoid forced layout
  reflows on every incoming message
- Use serialLines.slice(-maxLines) instead of repeated shift() (O(n²))
- Use replaceChildren() for single-DOM-operation node trimming

commit.message:
feat(dashboard): add simple fork picker to Add Project flow (#402)

* feat: Add fork picker to Add Project flow in dashboard

Add a 3-step wizard to the Add Project flow that allows users to:
1. Enter repository path/URL
2. Toggle 'This is a fork' option
3. Optionally enter fork owner/repo (or auto-detect from GitHub username)

This brings the --fork CLI flag functionality to the dashboard UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix fork auto-discovery and simplify fork picker UI

Bug #1: Auto-discovery was broken when user toggled 'This is a fork' but
left fork URL empty. The onAddProject callback incorrectly set forkValue
to opts.path, making both origin and upstream point to the same repo.

Fix: Pass 'auto' through to onboard() which now handles auto-discovery
for both forkWithoutPath AND fork==='auto' scenarios. The logic:
1. Gets GitHub username via 'gh api user --jq .login'
2. Extracts repo name from parsed URL or git remote
3. Constructs fork as username/reponame

Bug #2: The 3-step wizard flow was confusing (path → toggle → fork URL).

Fix: Simplified to 2-step flow (path → fork toggle). The fork toggle now
says 'Contributing via your fork?' with explanation text showing what
remotes will be set. Auto-detect is always used — no manual URL input.

Changes:
- lib/onboard.js: Handle fork==='auto' with path provided
- bin/rally.js: Pass fork value through unchanged (don't swap with path)
- lib/ui/components/OnboardInput.jsx: 2-step flow, clearer labels
- test/onboard-fork.test.js: Add test for fork=auto scenario

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(dashboard): add simple fork picker to Add Project flow

Adds a simple y/n fork prompt to the dashboard's Add Project screen:
1. User enters repo path
2. User types 'y' or 'n' to indicate if it's a fork
3. If fork, auto-detects GitHub username and configures remotes

Replaces the confusing multi-step wizard with a straightforward 2-step flow.

commit.message:
sumcheck: add SumcheckProof::update_claim helper for BDDT claim tracking (#473)

Add a public helper that reconstructs the round polynomial from BDDT
evaluation points [p(0), cubic_coeff, p(-1)] and evaluates it at the
verifier challenge via Horner's method, all in O(1) without allocating.

Works for both degree-3 (cubic) and degree-2 (quadratic, where
cubic_coeff = 0) claims, unifying the two previously separate inline
patterns.

Refactor Nova's own MemorySumcheckInstance and InnerBatchedSumcheckInstance
to use the new helper, removing ~20 lines of duplicated bookkeeping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

When multiple creatures share a tile, they were all drawn at the exact
same pixel position (tile center), making one visually disappear behind
the other. Now creatures sharing a tile are spread apart with small
pixel offsets so each remains individually visible.

- Updated tick() to group creatures by tile and apply offsets when stacked
- Added 4 regression tests covering single, double, and triple stacking

* fix: skip name prompt on reconnect when session is restored

The name prompt was shown unconditionally after every connect(), even when
the server successfully restored the player's identity from a previous
session via JWT token + persistence layer.

Fix: after connecting, check if the server restored a displayName from
the persistence layer. Only show the name prompt if displayName is empty
(first visit). On reconnect with a valid token, the player rejoins as
the same user with their previous displayName, score, XP, and level.

'Welcome back, {name}!' and others in the room see '{name} has returned'.

and added 25 to the already-restored score, causing a mismatch between
the displayed tile count and actual territory.

* feat: add game lobby — create, join, and manage game sessions (#83)

Introduces a lobby system that separates player identity from game state.
Players connect to a LobbyRoom first, then create or join specific games.

Server:
- LobbyRoom: Colyseus room managing game sessions (create/join/leave/start)
- LobbyState: Colyseus schema for lobby state (game list, connected players)
- GameSessionRepository: SQLite persistence for game session metadata
- GameRoom: now accepts per-game config (mapSize, seed, maxPlayers)

Client:
- LobbyScreen: DOM-based lobby UI with game list, create-game form, join flow
- network.ts: lobby-first connection (connectToLobby → joinGameRoom)
- main.ts: lobby shown first, game canvas hidden until game joined
- Name prompt moved to lobby layer (solves refresh re-prompt bug)

* fix: lobby lifecycle — zombie games, player count desync, disconnect cleanup

Address code review feedback on PR #84:

1. LobbyBridge: EventEmitter bridge for GameRoom → LobbyRoom communication.
   GameRoom emits player_count_changed and game_ended events that LobbyRoom
   subscribes to, fixing the missing inter-room lifecycle link.

2. Zombie games: GameRoom.onDispose now notifies LobbyRoom via bridge,
   which removes ended games from the lobby list and persists status.

3. Player count desync: GameRoom.onJoin/onLeave emit actual player counts
   via bridge, so LobbyRoom stays in sync with GameRoom reality.

4. Disconnect cleanup: LobbyRoom.onLeave now calls handleLeaveGame before
   removing the player, preventing ghost slots in game entries.

5. Multi-game guard: handleJoinGame rejects if player already has an
   activeGameId, preventing count inflation from spam joins.

6. Map size validation: Clamps mapSize to [32, 256] in handleCreateGame.

* fix: prevent same user from joining as two separate players

Multi-tab guard at two layers:

  older session (the new tab takes over).
- GameRoom.onJoin: If the same userId is already in the room, reject
  the join and notify the client. Prevents duplicate HQs, territory,
  and state persistence corruption from concurrent sessions.

stays visible in the lobby list.

commit.message:
[CBE Token Launch] Piecewise Linear Bonding Curve - Fix CBE Genesis Curve Type (#1854)

* feat(api): expose observer metrics via API endpoints (#1788)

Add API endpoints for consensus observer metrics and network health:

**Per-Height Metrics Endpoints:**
- GET /api/v1/observer/height/current - Current height metrics
- GET /api/v1/observer/height/{height} - Specific height metrics
  - round_count, commit_latency_ms, classification, phases

**Network Health Summary:**
- GET /api/v1/observer/network/health - Network-wide anomaly detection
  - stall_rate, average_rounds_per_height
  - partition_indicators (potential_partition_detected, stalled_heights, divergent_heights)
  - validator_anomaly_scores
  - recent_heights summary

**Surprisal Score (Anomaly Detection):**
- GET /api/v1/observer/surprisal/{height} - Anomaly score for height
  - surprisal_score, baseline_score, deviation_factor, classification

**Classification Logic:**
- healthy: 1 round, no stalls
- delayed: 1 round with stall
- recovering: 2-3 rounds, no fault
- degraded: 2-3 rounds with fault
- divergence: 4+ rounds

**Files Changed:**
- zhtp/src/api/handlers/observer.rs (new, 436 lines)
- zhtp/src/api/handlers/mod.rs (+observer module)
- zhtp/src/unified_server.rs (+ObserverHandler registration)

API contracts use stable JSON schemas with versioned structures.

Closes #1788

* fix(observer): normalize URI before route matching

Strip trailing slashes and query strings before dispatching to ensure
requests like /api/v1/observer/network/health?window=20 or with
a trailing slash are properly routed.

Addresses Copilot AI review comment on PR #1806.

* fix(observer): return 501 NotImplemented instead of synthetic data

The observer endpoints were returning synthetic/mock data (height-derived
round counts, hard-coded phase durations, fixed commit latency) instead
of real observer metrics.

Since the ObserverService from lib-consensus is not yet integrated into
the zhtp runtime, the endpoints now return proper HTTP 501 Not Implemented
with a clear message explaining that real observer data is pending.

Changes:
- All four endpoints return 501 NotImplemented
- Removed synthetic data generation helper methods
- Kept classify_height() as documentation of the API contract
- Added TODO comments explaining how to wire to real ObserverService:
  - get_height_analysis(height)
  - get_network_health(window_size)
  - surprisal_stats from HeightAnalysis

Addresses Copilot AI review comment on PR #1806.

* fix(monitor): normalize URI before route matching

Strip trailing slashes and query strings before dispatching to ensure
requests like /api/v1/monitor/health?verbose=1 or with a trailing
slash are properly routed.

Consistent with ObserverHandler and other API handlers.

Addresses Copilot AI review comment on PR #1806.

* Update zhtp/src/api/handlers/observer.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(monitor): add unit tests for MonitorHandler

Add comprehensive unit tests covering:
- can_handle logic for monitor paths
- Response schema structure validation
- Health status value constraints
- URI normalization logic

Note: Full integration tests require RuntimeOrchestrator which is
complex to set up in unit tests. Integration tests should be added
in zhtp/src/api/test_network_handler.rs pattern.

Addresses Copilot AI review comment on PR #1806.

* feat(bonding-curve): implement Issue #1820 - Bonding Curve Token Deployment via Consensus

- Add transaction handlers in process_token_transactions for:
  - BondingCurveDeploy: validates symbol uniqueness, creates token, registers in registry
  - BondingCurveBuy: executes buy with slippage protection, persists state
  - BondingCurveSell: executes sell with sell_enabled check, persists state
  - BondingCurveGraduate: validates CBE oracle gate, updates phase, persists state

- Refactor API endpoints to submit mempool transactions:
  - POST /api/v1/curve/deploy accepts signed BondingCurveDeploy tx
  - POST /api/v1/curve/buy accepts signed BondingCurveBuy tx
  - POST /api/v1/curve/sell accepts signed BondingCurveSell tx
  - POST /api/v1/curve/graduate accepts signed BondingCurveGraduate tx
  - All endpoints return { tx_hash, status: "submitted_to_mempool" }

- Add CBE genesis initialization:
  - Initializes CBE as built-in bonding curve token at node startup
  - Unblocks oracle price endpoints for CBE/USD

- Add zhtp-cli graduate command for CLI support

- Add 13 comprehensive integration tests covering all transaction types

- Fix duplicate classify_height function in observer.rs

* feat(bonding-curve): implement Issue #1820 core changes

- lib-blockchain: add process_token_transactions handlers for BondingCurveDeploy,
  BondingCurveBuy, BondingCurveSell, BondingCurveGraduate

* feat(pricing): implement Issue #1819 - Unified Token Pricing System (SOV + CBE)

## Summary
Implements unified token pricing system supporting both fixed (SRV) and
dynamic (oracle-derived) pricing modes for SOV and CBE tokens.

## Changes

### Phase 1: TokenPricingState Core
- Add lib-blockchain/src/pricing/mod.rs with unified pricing system
- TokenPricingState tracks CBE/USD oracle price, CBE/SOV ratio, pricing mode
- PricingCalculator for SOV/USD = CBE/USD ÷ CBE/SOV formula
- PricingMode enum: Fixed/Dynamic/PreGraduation/PostGraduation

### Phase 2: Unified Price API
- get_sov_price_unified(): Returns SOV price with mode indicator
  - Dynamic when both oracle signals available
  - Fixed (SRV) fallback when oracle not ready
- get_cbe_price_unified(): Returns CBE price with phase info
- Updated /api/v1/price/{token} endpoint

### Phase 3: Oracle Extension
- Extended OraclePriceAttestation with cbe_usd_price field
- Extended FinalizedOraclePrice with cbe_usd_price field
- Updated aggregation logic for dual-price finalization
- Auto-updates token_pricing_state when CBE price finalized

### Phase 4: Integration Tests
- Add lib-blockchain/tests/unified_pricing_tests.rs
- 8 comprehensive tests covering all functionality

## API Contract
GET /api/v1/price/sov returns:
{
  token_id: sov,
  symbol: SOV,
  price_usd_cents: 500,
  price_mode: dynamic,  // fixed or dynamic
  price_source: oracle_derived,
  confidence_level: high
}

## Backward Compatibility
- All CBE price fields are Option<T> with serde defaults
- Existing attestations without CBE price deserialize as None
- Fixed (SRV) pricing used as fallback

Closes #1819

* feat(pricing): integrate unified pricing across codebase

- Update API handlers for unified pricing endpoints

* feat(bonding-curve): implement Issue #1842 - Piecewise Linear Bonding Curve Mathematics

Implements document-compliant piecewise linear curve with 4 supply bands:
- Band 1: [0-10B] slope 2.5e-12, base 0.0003133457
- Band 2: [10B-30B] slope 7.5e-12
- Band 3: [30B-60B] slope 1.5e-11
- Band 4: [60B-100B] slope 3.0e-11

Features:
- Price continuity guaranteed at all band boundaries
- Proper fixed-point arithmetic using 10^16 combined scale
- Supports both positive and negative base offsets
- Integrated with existing CurveType enum as PiecewiseLinear variant
- Unit tests for continuity, price increase, and band detection

Note: Base values adjusted from spec to ensure continuity while
maintaining exact specification slopes.

Part of: CBE Token Launch #1841

* feat(bonding-curve): expose PiecewiseLinear in API and client

- Add PiecewiseLinear variant to CurveTypeRequest (API)
- Add From<CurveTypeRequest> for PiecewiseLinear using CBE defaults
- Re-export PiecewiseLinearCurve from bonding_curve module
- Add CURVE_TYPE_PIECEWISE_LINEAR constant (3) to lib-client
- Document all curve types in function signature

Part of: #1842, #1853

* Fix tests and add PiecewiseLinear support in oracle handler post-merge

- Updated bonding_curve_integration_tests.rs for CBE genesis token:
  * test_bonding_curve_full_lifecycle: expect 2 tokens (CBE + test)
  * test_multiple_tokens_different_phases: expect 4 tokens total, 2 in Curve
- Added PiecewiseLinear match arm in oracle/mod.rs base_price_atomic calculation
- Uses curve.price_at(0) as base price for variation calculations

* Update Cargo.lock post-merge

* fix(bonding-curve): use PiecewiseLinearCurve for CBE genesis token

The CBE genesis initialization was using a constant-price Linear curve,
but the specification requires the 4-band piecewise linear curve.

Changes:
- Import PiecewiseLinearCurve in initialize_cbe_genesis()
- Use CurveType::PiecewiseLinear(PiecewiseLinearCurve::cbe_default())
- Update documentation to reference Issue #1842

This ensures CBE follows the document-compliant pricing model from genesis.

Closes review finding: CBE genesis curve type mismatch

* fix(tools): remove missing remove_token bin target

The Cargo.toml referenced a remove_token.rs binary that doesn't exist,
causing workspace build failures. Removed the bin target entry.

This appears to be a development branch artifact where the bin target
was added but the source file was not committed.

- Add handle_graduate() method to CurveHandler
- Add route for POST /api/v1/curve/graduate
- Accepts signed_tx and returns placeholder response
- Full implementation pending (signature verification, graduation logic, AMM pool creation)

commit.message:
Fix workflow artifact upload paths missing "X" in EssentialsX addon jar names (#6462)

### Information

Workflow artifact upload steps referenced incorrect jar filenames — all
addon jars are built as `EssentialsX{Addon}-*.jar` (e.g.
`EssentialsXChat`, `EssentialsXProtect`) because project names in
`settings.gradle.kts` are all prefixed with `EssentialsX`, and
`archivesName` is set to `project.name`. The upload globs were missing
the `X`, causing every per-module direct upload to fail to find its
target file.

### Details

**Proposed fix:**  
Update all per-module `archive: false` upload step paths in both
`build-pr.yml` and `build-master.yml` to use the correct
`EssentialsX`-prefixed names:

| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `jars/EssentialsAntiBuild-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXAntiBuild-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsChat-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXChat-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsDiscord-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXDiscord-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsDiscordLink-*.jar` |
`jars/EssentialsXDiscordLink-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsGeoIP-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXGeoIP-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsProtect-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXProtect-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsSpawn-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXSpawn-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsXMPP-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXXMPP-*.jar` |

The Javadoc `cp` paths in `build-master.yml` reference filesystem
directory names (which don't include `X`) and remain correct as-is.

**Environments tested:**

OS: N/A (CI workflow change only)

Java version: N/A

- ~~Most recent Paper version (1.XX.Y, git-Paper-BUILD)~~
- ~~CraftBukkit/Spigot/Paper 1.12.2~~
- ~~CraftBukkit 1.8.8~~

**Demonstration:**

N/A — the incorrect globs would silently produce no artifact (or an
error) for each addon module on every workflow run using `archive:
false`.

commit.message:
feat: section header toggle (S) and timed style name flash on T

TUI tree:
- Press S to show/hide section header rows (Sub commands (N), Flags (N),
  Inherited flags (N), Positional arguments (N)). When hidden, all child
  items are shown flat without grouping dividers. State persists across
  navigation; press S again to restore headers.
- TreeModel.ToggleSections() / SectionsHidden() added to tree.go.
  flattenNode skips rowKindSection rows and always shows children when
  hideSections is true.

Status bar style flash:
- Pressing T to cycle display styles now shows the style name for
  cfg.StatusMsgTimeout (default 3 s) in purple, then automatically
  reverts to the normal key-hint text. Uses a tea.Tick / clearTimedMsgMsg
  to avoid blocking the render loop.
- Rapid T presses correctly reset the timer (expiry token prevents
  stale ticks from clearing a newer message early).
- Config.StatusMsgTimeout (time.Duration, default 3s) added to Config
  and DefaultConfig().

Status bar hints updated: added S:sections, removed redundant d:docs
(still reachable via d/D key, just not shown to keep hints concise).

commit.message:
refactor: Remove media deduplication, media always re-uploads

BREAKING: Removed the 'Clear media mappings' checkbox feature added in v0.17.9.
The deduplication logic was too complex and caused media migration failures.

Changes:
- Remove media mapping deduplication check in get_transferable_media_ids()
- Media now always collected and re-uploaded regardless of prior mappings
- Removed clear_media_mappings option from wizard payload
- Removed checkbox from migration UI (Step 3)
- Simplified logic: media always transfers, no smart deduplication

Why this matters:
- Media migration was completely broken due to old mappings filtering all media
- Pre-existing mappings from old migrations caused 0 media items to be migrated
- Deduplication was solving the wrong problem - just re-upload everything
- Simpler code, more reliable behavior, no configuration needed

Fixes issue where media phase showed 'completed' with items_total: 0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

names (created before IaC managed them). Hardcoding the existing GUIDs makes
Bicep perform idempotent PUTs instead of failing with RoleAssignmentExists.

function Reader were each assigned twice).

test: add concurrent stress tests, edge cases, and missing benchmarks (#23)

Add comprehensive edge-case coverage across all packages:

- response: 1MB body limit, nil data, truncated JSON, multiple status codes
- sanitize: Unicode/emoji filenames, long filenames, whitespace, IsDuplicateKey benchmark
- security: Mixed-case SQL/XSS detection, deeply nested PII, phone format variations,
  bool/nil values, array-of-maps, safe input verification
- middleware/brute_force: Concurrent RecordFailure/IsBlocked stress tests, benchmarks
- middleware/rate_limit: Concurrent request stress test, IPv6 XFF/RemoteAddr, XFF spacing
- middleware/jwt: Context overwrite, multi-setter chain, empty string auth edge case
- request: MaxInt64 boundary, overflow, large page offset, RequireURLParam benchmarks
- scan: Different generic type (intItem), close-on-scan-error verification
- health: Context cancellation checker, single checker test
- entitlements: TierLimits JSON roundtrip, TierConfig completeness, progressive features

commit.message:
fix: token rotation grace period to prevent session loss during deploys

Token rotation now uses a dedicated rotateRefreshToken() that only revokes
the specific old token instead of all user tokens. Added 30-second grace
period for revoked token reuse - if a new token was recently issued,
re-rotate instead of treating it as theft. This prevents session loss when
deploy interrupts the refresh response mid-flight.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove framer-motion from layout chain (~150KB off every page)

Navbar, Footer, NavDropdown, MobileSection, HamburgerIcon, and
MotionWrappers no longer import framer-motion. Replaced with:

- CSS transitions for dropdowns, mobile drawer, accordion sections
- CSS grid-rows trick for MobileSection expand/collapse
- IntersectionObserver + CSS classes for Footer reveal & AnimatedSection
- CSS keyframes for drawer slide-in and backdrop fade
- prefers-reduced-motion respected throughout

framer-motion now only loads on pages that truly need it (home hero,
dashboard, lab games) via lazy dynamic imports — not on every page.

commit.message:
fix: discovery parser no longer creates bogus subcommands from help text

- Map 'output formats', 'discovery strategies', 'interactive tui controls'
  and other free-text section headers to secDesc so they are never
  treated as subcommand lists
- Treat secExamples, secAliases, secDesc explicitly in the section switch
  (parse flags only; skip subcommand inference entirely)
- Add single-letter names (a-z) to skipSubcmdWords — hotkey hints like
  'h H  toggle help pane' no longer produce an 'h' subcommand
- Add ParseHelpOutputFor(text, selfName) that filters out subcommand
  names matching the CLI binary itself (prevents 'treemand' appearing as
  its own subcommand from example lines)
- Propagate selfName through discover() → ParseHelpOutputFor() call sites
- Fix CollapseAll to truly collapse everything (root included) so the
  row count is always 1 after collapse
- Add regression test TestParseHelpOutputFor_treemand_self that asserts
  'treemand treemand' produces only real subcommands

Print fidelity: pressure ink density + tilt print asymmetry (3G/3H)

3G  Pressure  ink density in print:
- Fiber ghosts: dashed center-line in ink color inside shallow cuts,
  simulating wood fibers that weren't fully cleared catching ink.
  Intensity inversely proportional to carve pressure.
- Ink edge pooling: thin dark outline at groove boundary on deep cuts,
  simulating ink collecting at sharp groove edges before transfer.
- Pressure-dependent edge noise: shallow cuts have rougher groove edges
  (28% width noise), deep cuts have clean edges (10%).

3H  Tilt  print asymmetry:
- Feathered edge on shallow wall: extended paper-color ribbon on the
  side opposite the gouge tilt, creating a soft transition.
  Enhanced tilt shift (1.6) pushes feathering outward.
- Rough wall fiber marks: thin dashed dark lines along the shallow
  edge, simulating fibers on the gradual wall catching ink.

All effects are print-only (forPrint flag); workspace rendering unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
